name: Chidi O.
style: Sharp, critical, specific about failures — names exact problems rather than generalising
tone: Frustrated and demanding; expects baseline standards to be met as a minimum
themes: service speed, food quality, hygiene, value for money

reviews.text: Deceptive business practices.  Paid for ONE year, agreed to one year, no continuing service.  They came out anyway with no warning, sprayed only the front yard and sent me a bill for double what I paid the previous year.  DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH TRUGREEN.  Even for the one year I did agree to have them service the lawn, still loads of weeds and ...

Really not a good experience.  Ordered a part for my MB, and the parts department was great, but when it wouldn't fit correctly, the parts department suggested I see if someone from service could take a look and verify I wasn't doing something wrong.    That's when it all changed.  Instead of taking 5 minutes and looking at it himself, offering ...

This used to be a wonderful dining experience, but what I had on 3/17 could best be described as expensive Golden Corral.  Service was atrocious, despite reservations we had to wait a significant amount of time to be seated and our table was pretty much in the main walkway so I was dodging plates over my head half the night.  Sides were cold, ne...

Went here for lunch to get the prime rib sandwich that my fiancee has been talking about since she had it at a company party awhile back.  I also ordered the tomato and mozzarella (caprese) salad.    The sandwich was phenomenal.  Obviously a bit expensive being that this is a very upscale place, but honestly worth the money.  Au jus was fantasti...

This is my favorite Mexican place in the city.  Started going when it was still a hole in the wall across from the dollar theater.  Tacos are a fantastic deal, and the queso is amazing.  Their homemade salsa loses a lot of heat on the weekends, but mid week it's nice and hot.

I may be a bit biased working for AT&T business marketing, but this location and circle center are my favorite stores to visit when I need help with my personal service or need upgrades, accessories, etc.  short waits, quality reps here.

This is THE place to take any european car in Indianapolis.  I've had two Mercedes, meticulously maintained by Euro Motorworks.  Their pricing is fair (and much less than the dealer), they have loaners available so you're not stuck there all day, and the most important - they know enough about the cars to give an honest assessment of what's in t...

name: Blessing A.
style: Enthusiastic and exclamatory; uses exclamation marks freely; focuses on highlights
tone: Genuinely excited, warm and grateful — reads as a friend recommending something they loved
themes: atmosphere, staff warmth, memorable moments, food presentation

reviews.text: Got sick with food poisoning from the salmon sashimi. I ordered a tuna handroll, got a tuna roll with a side cucumber handroll... NOT what i ordered. The fish was so sour, i could only eat one bite of each. The check took forever to come. I LOVE sushi and i HATED the fish here... im  still sick from it. HORRIBLE experience will NEVER go back! Th...
went back! excellent customer service from chris! love this store!
LOVE Lush, Excellent customer service! Get a Bubble Bar!

The interior features a beautiful speakeasy decor. The speciality cocktails are the best on bourbon st. I actually dined here for the first time tonight and I would highly recommend the shrimp & grits! Love this place!

Wooo Child! Love this place. So upscale, excellent drinks, and i love the owners & staff. I started out as a 'regular' right when it opened, i moved about a year later & i always get a warm welcome when i come to visit. You must check this place out, extremely clean, smoke-free establishment. Try the mojitos! 5 stars!!!

Beautiful display of gorgeous pastries, cakes and macaroons. Stunning wedding cake displayed upon entry. Staff were very warm and welcoming. I enjoyed my 3 items, I just wish the topping on the carrot cake was sweeter (I've got a major sweet tooth) but everything else was 10/10. Fresh and affordable, I will be back!

name: Emmanuel T.
style: Analytical and balanced; often lists pros then cons; uses measured qualifiers
tone: Measured and fair — gives credit where due, notes flaws without exaggerating
themes: overall value, consistency, whether it meets expectations

reviews.text: This place is not great.   We were stuck in a table under the stairs, the hot pink wall combined with a too strong chandelier, left us feeling like we were dining in a soviet interrogation room.   The server took forever to come over for the first time.  I think they forgot about us since we were hidden.  The teriyaki was good as was the sushi, ...
This is affordable decent Chinese food.  Nothing special, but everything is done well and the food arrives hot and the order is accurate.

Ribs are amazing!!!! Like orgasmic, dream inducing, crave worthy delicious.  Everything else is just OK.  Potato salad was  acceptable Green beans were bad Fried. Corn was OK Beef brisket was mediocre  Ribs were amazing. Go here for the ribs and everything else is bonus.
It's the best. I make a pilgrimage for Los Angeles at least once a year for this.  Amazing.

This bakery is a family tradition!  Literally, I am the fourth generation fan and I come all the way from Los Angeles for their bakery goodness.  I have bought occasion cakes as well as morning pastries and it is all delicious.   GO there NOW!  Pros:  Everything they bake is perfect  Cons:  limited hours cash only

I came here with my friend as his guest and I was blown away. Literally.  The fans are HUGE!  The gym was clean and the air flow from those giant fans made working out a cool experience.   Memberships prices are reasonable and everything is brand new.   A+

name: Sarah K.
style: Very short sentences. Minimal elaboration. States facts and moves on.
tone: Direct and no-nonsense — says exactly what needs saying, nothing more

reviews.text: Ambiance: 4/5 Food: 2/5 Service: 2/5 Value: 1/5
Ambiance: 4/5 Food: 3/5 Service: 3/5 Value: 3.5/5
Ambiance: 1/5 Food: 4/5 Service: 4/5 Value: 2.5/5
Ambiance: 4/5 Food: 5/5 Service: 4/5 Value: 3/5
Ambiance: 4/5 Food: 4/5 Service: 3/5 Value: 3/5
Stopped by The Ridge on vacation when we got tired of waiting for Mattie B's. Amazing food, hospitality, and prices at this hole-in-the-wall hub! Will definitely be back for a future visit.

name: Funmilayo A.
style: Narrative storytelling with vivid sensory detail; sets the scene before the review
tone: Warm and immersive — draws you into the experience rather than just describing it
themes: ambiance, sensory experience, the full arc of the visit

reviews.text: I've long admired the Hotel Mazarin for its picturesque courtyard because I'm often in the adjoining wine bar, the exquisite Patrick's Bar Vin. I've spend many an idyllic afternoon/evening here. I've also booked several of my colleagues at the Mazarin when they've come to New Orleans and heard good things from them (as recently as January 2015)....

The simply-named Tavern offers up some mighty simple comfort food pleasures (White Trash hash, anyone?) and elevated junk food game bites.   That said, I'm not sure this is quite the weekend brunch place for me, due to the TVs providing all of the background noise. I'm not a fan of big TVs blaring games -- to me, this is the worst kind of ambian...

It seems that true vintage shops are themselves becoming a thing of the past. Where there were once specific streets or neighborhoods that were known to be great for vintage shopping in any given city, that's no longer the case in cities like New Orleans or New York. I look at the 50+ dresses that are in my closet, mainly from the 20s through th...

The Tiki gods have answered my prayers! New Orleans now has a fabulous Tiki bar, with just the right dark Polynesian atmosphere that's straight outta Trader Vics circa 1965. My mom would always regale me with stories about her times at Hawaii Kai in Manhattan in the 60s. As a child, I'd cherish our annual trips to Mai Kai in Fort Lauderdale (whi...

Thanks for the reminder, Yelp! I love Fair Grinds, yet I apparently never wrote them a review. So here's the five star review they've been missing for almost 2 years now.   First a mention on how they are one of the only coffeehouses in NOLA that's dedicated to fair trade coffees and all of their tea is organic. That the tea is organic is a huge...

Give me a bottle of red wine and a backyard, throw in some good company and/or good music and I really can't really be much happier. Hello, Bacchanal!  Oh, but there's more! See, what you're doing is buying the bottle at the price you'd get it at the wine shop (essentially because you *are* at a wine shop), so there's no bar/lounge mark up, then...

name: Marcus B.
style: Long, comprehensive, well-structured with context; references specific dishes by name
tone: Authoritative and thorough — reads like a considered assessment, not a quick reaction
themes: ingredient quality, technique, service professionalism, comparison to peers

reviews.text: This was a fun place for the most part. Day one: it was a breezy day and the water was also freezing, so less enjoyable. Day two: warmer day and warmer water. We got what we went there for and had some fun so can't complain. I wish there was more outdoor stuff.

I'm torn on how to review this place as I have mixed feelings. The concept was much nicer than the reality IMO but it wasn't bad. While two of four of us felt our dinner dishes were "meh" they were still decent and the other two were very happy. The beer selection was great. It's a tad pretentious perhaps. Spent way more than I would have liked ...

This was a very pleasant experience. The room was big and clean, nice kitchen too. The hotel has an indoor pool, fitness room, tennis court and free laundry for guests. Everyone was friendly and even the breakfast was good. I would definitely stay here again.

Not my best experience and I am a big fan usually. They forgot the biscuits, the gravy and the grits. My eggs were prepared wrong and the server while helpful was not generally pleasant. Being seated in 15 minutes on a Saturday at lunchtime was a plus.
Like any other PF Changs this was no different. The food was exactly what we expected and service was fast and friendly. We had a reservation and were seated immediately upon checking in.

name: Ngozi P.
style: Casual and conversational with dry humour; short paragraphs; unexpectedly witty observations
tone: Funny and relatable — makes you laugh while still making a genuine point
themes: the gap between expectation and reality, absurd details, staff personalities

reviews.text: I wasn't so hungry but my boyfriend was starving so he ordered pho & I ate the summer rolls.  The pho was okay -- good enough to get you full but didn't leave you deliciously satisfied.  I tried a bit of his & I wasn't overwhelmed by it.  The summer rolls -- I had the pork and shrimp -- were pretty lackluster.  They were just these floppy rice r...

When I first moved to the area, I was super excited that there was an Indian restaurant within walking distance.  I absolutely love Indian food!  My bf & I went there and ordered the chicken tikka masala and chicken korma.  We were so excited when the entrees came but were immediately disappointed.  I've never had Indian food that tasted so mehh...

I needed to use my groupon deal before it expired so we came here on a Friday night.  I guess a lot of people bought the deal bc there were tons of signs by the entrance advising to tell your hostess if you're using the deal & that you would have to pay in cash (are they allowed to do that?)  Regardless, they were super friendly and welcoming.  ...

Finally had the chance to try out this place & I will def be a regular customer now.  Amazing selection of hot dogs that are absolutely loaded with toppings & reasonably priced.    I wasn't feeling too hungry so I had the Fiesta dog which came on a hearty & toasted bun, guacamole, lettuce, tomatoes, & sour cream.  It was filling and fresh -- fel...

Bistro Romano has amazing decor!  Once you walk in, you feel like you've been instantly transported to a beautiful, romantic, cozy brick wine cellar.   We started off with the calamari and the clams/mussels appetizer.  The calamari was perfect - crispy and not greasy at all.  I only wish they gave us more.  The clams/mussels were decent but I wo...

I came here not really knowing to expect from a Peruvian/Asian fusion restaurant but I'm happy to say I was very very satisfied with Chifa.  Chifa has a classy Asian flare to its decor.  It's dark, private, & quite spacious.  The front is partitioned off with curtains while the back has unique gazebolike partitions.  Either way, you'll be sittin...

name: David O.
style: Emotional and personal; anchors reviews in specific memories or people
tone: Warm and reflective — connects food to life moments and relationships
themes: personal meaning, memories triggered, emotional resonance of the experience

reviews.text: Oh, Passe to India, how you confused us.  "Great Indian food" says a literature source.    Hmm, reality.....food is pretty good, not great, but maybe Boston spoiled us.  Atmosphere here is....lacking.  Character it has!   The dining area is tiered, almost dining hall feel to it.  There was some fairly annoying TV show going on in the corner whic...

Buckets is an interesting place.  I have yet to see the place have more than about 4 people in it, unless I was in the group of 4+ that walks into the bar.  This may mean I am completely out of touch with then it "the time" to go to Buckets, but it does mean that you can always grab a seat, play some pool (or be in a VERY short line for pool) an...

Go figure that Bry takes my "go to" plan and order the pork and doesn't enjoy it.  I get a steak and might leave her for it.  This place had a smaller menu than I would have liked, but for the most part (just counting her "bad" pork, I liked it)  the food was great.  Good cocktail selection, nice wine selection, GREAT atmosphere.   Fun space tuc...

The Buffalo Chicken Pizza here maaaay be the best novelty pizza that I've ever put in my mouth.  I really like the menu options, even if I will never take advantage of most of them.  Couch Tomato Cafe has a good, customizable selection of salads, wraps, and pizzas.  The atmosphere is a little fun, with couches (duh, their name) and bar style win...

I realllllly wanted to like the house brews here.  I can tell you that they are all very rich and distinct flavors, but for the most part I didn't like them.  This is VERY subjective, and I'm sure many people will like these distinct flavors.   I don't like cauliflower either, so sue me.  The menu has some great food options.  The French Dip san...

The Clearview Bala Theatre is a testament to an older, smaller City of Philadelphia. A city where going to a show (not a movie here, but a play or something *gasp* "live") was a primary source of social activity and relaxation.   You will not find your first-run box-office smashes here - but you will find second-run viewings and independent movi...
